scope.note: Scope is domain-bound by default: the source URL carries the domain, and the domain bounds the authority. A concept is admissible only if its source domain falls within these domains. Widening beyond the default (kubernetes.io) is explicit and recorded here.

note: Curated operational concepts. Each concept must carry a source (within scope) and a resolves-check; a concept without both is not knowledge. Validity is conferred by a human signature on the built Box, not by this file.

concepts.signal: Pod STATUS=ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull; alert KubeContainerWaiting
concepts.context: A workload's pods never reach Running; container stays Waiting.
causes: image/tag does not exist in the registry
private registry with no imagePullSecret
registry auth/rate-limit failure
wrong registry host or repository path
resolution: kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # read the exact Waiting reason + Events
confirm the image:tag exists in the registry (or publish it)
if private: create a docker-registry secret and reference it via imagePullSecrets
correct image.repository / image.tag if malformed
concepts.resolves: kubectl get pod shows Running; the KubeContainerWaiting alert clears

concepts.context: Container starts, exits non-zero, is restarted with exponential backoff.
causes: application panics or exits on boot
missing/invalid config or env
failing liveness probe killing a healthy-but-slow container
missing dependency or unreachable downstream at startup
resolution: kubectl -n NS logs POD --previous  # logs from the crashed instance
kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # exit code + Events
fix config/secret/env or correct the boot fault
if liveness is too aggressive, raise initialDelaySeconds/failureThreshold
concepts.resolves: restart count stabilizes; pod becomes Ready

concepts.context: The scheduler cannot place the pod on any node.
causes: insufficient CPU/memory on nodes
no node matches nodeSelector/affinity
node taints without matching tolerations
unbound PersistentVolumeClaim
topology spread / PodDisruptionBudget constraints
resolution: kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # read FailedScheduling reason
add capacity, relax nodeSelector/affinity, or add tolerations
ensure the PVC binds (StorageClass / available PV)
concepts.resolves: pod is assigned a node; transitions ContainerCreating -> Running

concepts.context: The container exceeded its memory limit and was killed by the kernel.
causes: memory limit set below real working-set
memory leak or unbounded buffering
load spike beyond provisioned headroom
resolution: kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # confirm OOMKilled
raise resources.limits.memory (and requests) to the observed working-set
fix the leak / cap in-process buffering
concepts.resolves: no further OOMKilled events under representative load

concepts.context: The kubelet cannot build the container because a referenced object is missing.
causes: referenced ConfigMap or Secret does not exist in the namespace
referenced key missing from the ConfigMap/Secret
wrong namespace for the reference
resolution: kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # names the missing object/key
create the ConfigMap/Secret, or fix the env/volume reference
verify the object is in the pod's namespace
concepts.resolves: container is created; pod proceeds to Running

concepts.signal: Pod Running but READY 0/1; Service endpoints empty
concepts.context: The container runs but its readiness probe never succeeds, so it receives no traffic.
causes: probe path/port wrong
app not listening on the probed port yet (initialDelay too short)
dependency the readiness check requires is down
resolution: kubectl -n NS describe pod POD  # Readiness probe failures in Events
verify the probe path/port match the server (e.g. /readyz on the http port)
raise initialDelaySeconds; fix the dependency the check gates on
concepts.resolves: pod becomes READY 1/1; it appears in the Service's endpoints

concepts.context: The kubelet evicted pods to reclaim a starved node resource.
causes: node out of memory or disk (ephemeral storage)
no resource requests, so pods are first to be evicted
log/emptyDir growth filling the node
resolution: kubectl describe node NODE  # confirm the pressure condition
set resource requests so pods get a QoS class above BestEffort
free node disk / cap log volume; add capacity
concepts.resolves: pods stay scheduled; no new Evicted events

concepts.context: An init container has not completed, so app containers never start.
causes: init container waits on an unreachable dependency
init container itself errors/crashes
volume the init step needs is not ready
resolution: kubectl -n NS logs POD -c INIT_CONTAINER  # init container logs
fix the dependency the init step blocks on, or the init command itself
concepts.resolves: init completes; app containers start and reach Running
